rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE AUSTIN ERIK HILLS 2013 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To File Under Seal (Ca Rule Of Court 2.551) (Redacted)
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to File Under Seal is denied. A record may be filed under seal only if the court expressly finds facts that establish that (1) an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and, (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (CRC, Rule 2.550(d)). The proposed sealing is not narrowly tailored; it seeks to seal the successor trustee's entire supplemental declaration and redacted attorney timesheets previously submitted with the First Account. The timesheets are not submitted with this Motion, so the Court cannot evaluate the claim to confidentiality beyond noting that there is no blanket privilege for timesheets, which are routinely submitted in support of fee requests as required by local rule so that the Court can determine whether the requested fees are reasonable. The supplemental declaration shares information which the Motion argues should be sealed, as to which the Court cannot find a private interest sufficient to overcome the right of public access and much of which is not directly related to the accounting-including the successor trustee's opinions about the beneficiary and his motivations, descriptions of the beneficiary's medical state, advice from the beneficiary's medical care provider. There is no showing that the Trust's payment of the beneficiary's attorney's fees or other unexpected expenses is an overriding interest overcoming the right of public access or that that there is a substantial probability that any such interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed. The Motion does not establish how reference to the existence of a settlement agreement between the successor trustee and an unnamed third party without discussion of its terms would breach such a settlement's confidentiality clause. In any event, parties cannot override the standards of Rule 2.550 by private agreement. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and other parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, February 19, 2020.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Oral Stipulation Or In The Alternative, To Set Aside The Spousal Property Order
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Enforce Oral Stipulation or in the Alternative Vacate Order is granted in the alternative. The motion does not present, and the Court is not aware of, authority that would allow the Court to enforce by motion an oral settlement agreement resolving objections to a spousal property petition that the Court has already granted. The settlement agreement, even assuming that it could be enforced as a binding oral contract through a separate proceeding, does not satisfy the requirements of CCP section 664.6 for expedited enforcement by motion in the underlying action for a variety of reasons including that it is not written, not signed by the parties, and not incorporated into a court order retaining enforcement jurisdiction. Objector's motion requested that if the Court did not grant its request to enforce the settlement, it should vacate the order that was granted on the basis of the settlement. Petitioner does not object to that relief. Therefore, the August 21, 2019 Order granting the spousal property petition is vacated. Petitioner is ordered promptly to reverse and undo any actions taken on the basis of the Court's August 21, 2019 Order, including the recording of that order. Hearing on the spousal property petition and opposition/objection to it is reset for April 7, 2020. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, February 19, 2020.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Thomas Wrobel, Esq. For $6650.00
rulings.Rulings: The motion for sanctions is denied. Attorney Wrobel recited the terms of a settlement agreement and represented in open court that his client agreed to sell or refinance the subject property within eight months, in exchange for which promise Objector agreed to withdraw immediately objections to the spousal property petition, and, as a result, Petitioner obtained his spousal property order that day instead of at some later time. Petitioner himself was in court during these proceedings. Attorney Wrobel then denied having made the agreement on behalf of his client and denied that his client obtained relief on the basis of an agreement. Counsel either failed to obtain his client's consent to the agreement or misrepresented that consent to the Court and opposing party. The Court finds bad faith in either or both of the recital of an agreement without intending to abide by it in order to obtain desired relief and/or the denial of that agreement as soon as the relief was obtained. Nevertheless, sanctions are not appropriate on this record because Petitioner offered to vacate the order, the very relief Objector will obtain through this motion, prior to the motion being filed. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, February 19, 2020.
